title: Build a reliable evaluation runner/orchestrator for feedback loops
description:
## Summary
Build a reliable runner/orchestrator for feedback optimization loops.

## Deliverables
- Start evaluation and capture the evaluation ID immediately.
- Wait on backend evaluation status rather than trusting local CLI exit.
- Collect final metrics and RCA.
- Write a standardized Kanbus comment for each run.
- Treat the evaluation record as the source of truth for run state.

- Reliable verification pattern is to read evaluation state from  after dispatch; do not infer success from local process behavior alone.
- Confirmed recent successful run for validation: evaluation  (COMPLETED, processedItems=20, RCA present).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Cold-restart runbook (authoritative): (1) Run evaluations via repo-local module, not stale installed binary: PYTHONPATH=/Users/ryan/Projects/Plexus /Users/ryan/miniconda3/bin/python -m plexus.cli.shared.CommandLineInterface evaluate feedback --scorecard-name 'CMG EDU' --score-name 'Identify Objections' --score-version-id db3df9e6-2f78-4f0a-9876-48bd16515cdd --number-of-days 180 --max-samples 100 --account-key call-criteria --scorecard-key cmg-edu --task-id <KANBUS_TASK>. (2) Treat evaluation record as source of truth; verify with MCP plexus_evaluation_info by evaluation id. (3) Success criteria for loop runs: status COMPLETED, processedItems==totalItems, AC1 present, root_cause persisted when incorrect items exist. (4) Known-good completion example after fixes: eval 1baec889-41e3-45c8-9e06-3ebda9458bda.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting next under feature/plx-next-data-flywheel: implement reliable feedback-evaluation runner/orchestrator path (start -> wait -> collect final metrics + RCA -> standardized Kanbus comment) using evaluation record as source of truth.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented reliable feedback-evaluation runner/orchestrator path.

Delivered:
- Added shared module plexus/cli/shared/feedback_evaluation_runner.py with one canonical flow:
  1) spawn plexus evaluate feedback with deterministic runner task_id,
  2) discover evaluation_id by task_id via account-scoped evaluation query,
  3) wait for terminal backend evaluation status via Evaluation.get_evaluation_info,
  4) build standardized run summary,
  5) optional standardized Kanbus comment write (kbs comment) when issue id provided.
- Added new CLI command plexus evaluate feedback-runner (orchestrated wrapper) exposing timeout/poll controls and optional --kanbus-issue-id.
- Updated MCP plexus_evaluation_run feedback path to use task-id-correlated orchestration (no latest-evaluation polling).

Validation:
- Added tests: plexus/cli/shared/feedback_evaluation_runner_test.py (6 passing).
- Passed compile checks for modified modules with python -m py_compile.

This satisfies plx-9aa370 deliverables: capture evaluation ID, wait on backend status, collect final metrics+RCA, standardized Kanbus run summary.
